story.soThat: I can mock database interactions in my unit tests

task.title: Analyze existing repository implementations and extract interface contracts

subtasks.subtask: Review sdk/endor_resource_repository.go and related repository files
Identify all public methods used by EndorService and EndorHybridService
Extract common CRUD patterns: Create, Read, Update, Delete, List, Query operations
Design generic-friendly interfaces with type parameters for resource types
Define RepositoryInterface with core CRUD operations
Define ResourceRepositoryInterface with endor-specific resource operations
Add comprehensive GoDoc documentation with usage examples
Add interface compliance tests to sdk/interfaces_test.go
Create unit tests demonstrating repository interface usage patterns
Verify existing repository implementations satisfy new interfaces
Test generic type safety with concrete resource types
Review current error handling in repository implementations
Define domain error types that abstract away MongoDB specifics
Update interface signatures to use domain errors
Ensure error semantics remain clear for framework users

criterion.title: Interface Definition
criterion.description: RepositoryInterface, ResourceRepositoryInterface, and MongoRepositoryInterface are defined in sdk/interfaces/repository.go

criterion.title: Method Abstraction
criterion.description: All current repository methods are abstracted into interfaces with domain-focused signatures

criterion.title: Implementation Compliance
criterion.description: MongoDB repository implementations satisfy their respective interfaces

criterion.title: Generic Type Safety
criterion.description: Repository interfaces support generic types for compile-time type safety

criterion.title: Domain Error Handling
criterion.description: Interface methods return domain errors rather than database-specific errors

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.snippet: Developers can unit test services without MongoDB dependencies through interface mocking. Database access occurs through repository interfaces that can be mocked. Developers can provide custom repository implementations for testing.

artifact.title: Refactored Architecture Document
artifact.snippet: Interface Segregation with Smart Composition. Following Go's interface philosophy of "accept interfaces, return structs" with RepositoryInterface for data access and composed larger interfaces when needed.

artifact.title: Epic Breakdown
artifact.snippet: Framework components become mockable and testable. FR4: All framework components implement well-defined interfaces for easy mocking. FR5: Unit testing without MongoDB dependencies.

artifact.title: Epic Technical Specification: Interface Foundation & Testability
artifact.snippet: Create RepositoryInterface[T] and ResourceRepositoryInterface[T] for all data access patterns. Generic data access abstraction with entity types and specialized repository for Endor resources with categories.

artifact.title: Story 1.1: Extract Core Service Interfaces
artifact.snippet: Interface Package Created: sdk/interfaces/service.go established - extend with repository.go. Testing Patterns: Interface compliance tests using var _ Interface = (*Implementation)(nil) pattern. No Breaking Changes: All existing APIs preserved.

artifact.reason: Existing generic repository interface with CRUD operations for ResourceInstance[T] that needs to be moved to interfaces package
Specialized repository interface for category-based resources that needs interface abstraction
Static repository interface for direct model type access that requires interface extraction
Core service repository with Create, UpdateOne, DeleteOne methods that need interface abstraction
MongoDB implementation that will need to satisfy extracted repository interfaces
MongoDB static repository implementation requiring interface compliance
Data transfer objects used in repository method signatures
Existing interface package where repository interfaces will be added following established patterns
Existing testing patterns for interface compliance using var _ Interface = (*Implementation)(nil)

package.name: go.mongodb.org/mongo-driver
package.usage: MongoDB driver for database operations in repository implementations

package.name: github.com/stretchr/testify
package.usage: Testing framework for interface compliance tests and mocking

package.name: github.com/gin-gonic/gin
package.usage: HTTP framework for context handling in EndorContext

architectural.constraint: Follow Interface Segregation Principle with Smart Composition as defined in architecture document
Implement "accept interfaces, return structs" Go philosophy for repository layer
Maintain generic type safety using Go 1.21+ generics for compile-time validation
Preserve all existing EndorService and EndorHybridService public API contracts

development.constraint: Interface extraction must not break existing repository implementations
All new interfaces must be placed in sdk/interfaces/repository.go following established package structure
Interface compliance tests must use var _ Interface = (*Implementation)(nil) pattern
Domain error types must abstract away MongoDB-specific error details

testing.constraint: Interface compliance tests required for all MongoDB repository implementations
Generic type safety must be validated with concrete resource types
Error handling tests must verify domain error abstraction works correctly

interface.name: RepositoryInterface[T]
interface.signature: Instance(ctx context.Context, dto ReadInstanceDTO) (*ResourceInstance[T], error) List(ctx context.Context, dto ReadDTO) ([]ResourceInstance[T], error) Create(ctx context.Context, dto CreateDTO[ResourceInstance[T]]) (*ResourceInstance[T], error) Delete(ctx context.Context, dto ReadInstanceDTO) error Update(ctx context.Context, dto UpdateByIdDTO[ResourceInstance[T]]) (*ResourceInstance[T], error)

interface.name: ResourceRepositoryInterface[T, C]
interface.signature: Instance(ctx context.Context, dto ReadInstanceDTO) (*ResourceInstanceSpecialized[T, C], error) List(ctx context.Context, dto ReadDTO) ([]ResourceInstanceSpecialized[T, C], error) Create(ctx context.Context, dto CreateDTO[ResourceInstanceSpecialized[T, C]]) (*ResourceInstanceSpecialized[T, C], error) Delete(ctx context.Context, dto ReadInstanceDTO) error Update(ctx context.Context, dto UpdateByIdDTO[ResourceInstanceSpecialized[T, C]]) (*ResourceInstanceSpecialized[T, C], error)

interface.name: StaticRepositoryInterface[T]
interface.signature: Instance(ctx context.Context, dto ReadInstanceDTO) (T, error) List(ctx context.Context, dto ReadDTO) ([]T, error) Create(ctx context.Context, dto CreateDTO[T]) (T, error) Delete(ctx context.Context, dto ReadInstanceDTO) error Update(ctx context.Context, dto UpdateByIdDTO[T]) (T, error)

interface.name: ServiceRepositoryInterface
interface.signature: Create(dto CreateDTO[Resource]) error UpdateOne(dto UpdateByIdDTO[Resource]) (*Resource, error) DeleteOne(dto ReadInstanceDTO) error

standards.paragraph: Testing follows layered strategy with build tags separating unit tests (//go:build unit) from integration tests (//go:build integration). Unit tests use interface mocks without external dependencies while integration tests validate real database interactions. Interface compliance tests use var _ Interface = (*Implementation)(nil) pattern established in sdk/interfaces_test.go. All tests leverage testify framework for assertions and behavior verification. Repository interfaces enable comprehensive error scenario testing through mock implementations.

locations.location: sdk/interfaces_test.go - Interface compliance tests for repository interfaces
test/ - Unit and integration tests using new interface-based patterns
sdk/testutils/ - Mock implementations and test utilities (from Story 1.4)

idea.title: Repository Interface Compliance Tests
idea.description: Verify all MongoDB repository implementations satisfy their respective interfaces using var _ RepositoryInterface[TestEntity] = (*MongoResourceInstanceRepository[TestEntity])(nil) pattern

idea.title: Generic Type Safety Validation
idea.description: Test repository interfaces with concrete resource types to ensure generic type constraints work correctly and compile-time type safety is preserved

idea.title: Implementation Compliance Integration Tests
idea.description: Integration tests that verify MongoDB implementations handle all CRUD operations correctly through the interface contracts

idea.title: Domain Error Abstraction Tests
idea.description: Unit tests verifying interface methods return domain errors (NotFoundError, ValidationError) instead of MongoDB-specific errors (mongo.ErrNoDocuments)

idea.title: Repository Interface Usage Pattern Tests
idea.description: Tests demonstrating how EndorService and EndorHybridService interact with repository interfaces, validating the extracted method signatures match current usage patterns
